# Canva thumbnail brief – Dr. Reddy videos

Use this in **Canva** (or Figma, Adobe Express) for faster, higher-fidelity thumbnails.

## Specs
- **Size:** 1280 × 720 px (YouTube thumbnail)
- **Face asset:** Use `Dr-Reddy-qr-code.png` from this repo (same folder as this doc, or `/wellnest/`). Crop so his face is prominent; add a **white or light glow/outline** on the edge where the face meets the text panel so he “pops.”
- **Export:** PNG, 1280×720. Name files exactly: `video-reddy-1.png` … `video-reddy-16.png` and drop them into this folder so the app picks them up.

---

## Template styles (pick per thumbnail)

### Style A – Black panel + key word in banner
- **Left or right:** Face on one side (with glow), **solid black** on the other.
- **Text:** Top line (white, bold). Middle = **one key word/phrase in a colored rectangle** (red, green, or your choice). Optional third line below (e.g. “WHICH TO USE?”).
- **Inspiration:** Skincare-style thumbnails with “PEPTIDES” in a red banner.

### Style B – Stacked two-tone banners
- Face on one side (with glow). Other side = solid color (blue, teal, red, etc.).
- **Two stacked rectangles:** Line 1 on **black**, line 2 on **red or yellow**. Rounded corners.
- Optional: **Red arrow** pointing from text toward the face.

### Style C – Face + colored panel + outline
- Face one side (with glow), solid color panel other side.
- **Two lines of bold text** (no banners). Thin **light blue or white border** around the whole frame.

---

---

## Canva workflow (fast)

1. **Create one design:** 1280×720, add Dr. Reddy photo, add text and shapes for **Style A**.
2. **Duplicate** the design 16 times (Duplicate page or Copy design).
3. **Swap text** in each copy using the table above. Change the key-word banner color per row if you like.
4. **Export** each page as PNG, 1280×720, named `video-reddy-1.png` … `video-reddy-16.png`.
5. **Save** all 16 into `public/wellnest/thumbnails/`.

## Other tools
- **Figma:** Same dimensions; use components for “key word banner” and “stacked banners” so you only edit text.
- **Adobe Express:** Same idea; use templates and replace text/photo.
- **Photopea** (free, in-browser): Like a light Photoshop; good if you want layers and precise control without a subscription.

The HTML generator in this repo (`thumbnail-generator.html`) is still there if you want a quick preview or fallback; Canva will give you better type, effects, and consistency once you have one template you like.
